mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. This signal is always active and does not require a threshold condition.
failure_condition: If over any rolling 2-year window the rank IC between RSAFS month-over-month growth and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is below 0.03 for a sustained period of 6 months, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 2.5%, it signifies tightening monetary policy that increases borrowing costs and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average 60-day forward return following at least 5 trigger events is not significantly negative (one-sample t-test p > 0.10), the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declining unemployment and rising retail sales indicate a strong economy with tight labor markets boosting consumer spending, which positively impacts S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The joint signal is stronger than the sum of individual effects.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE decline > 0.2pp over 3 months AND RSAFS growth > 1% month-over-month
failure_condition: If in a linear regression of 20-day returns on UNRATE change, RSAFS growth, and their interaction, the interaction coefficient is not significantly positive (p < 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window, then reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime characterized by industrial production growth slowing, negative surprises in INDPRO lead to downward revisions in earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. In early-cycle recovery the same signal may be positive, but the regime-conditional effect is negative when late-cycle conditions hold.
failure_condition: If the conditional mean 20-day return following the trigger in late-cycle regimes (defined by GDP growth above trend and rising CPI) is not significantly more negative than in early-cycle regimes (two-sample t-test p > 0.10), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in nonfarm payrolls signals a healthy labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days. This is an always-on trend signal.
failure_condition: If the rank IC of PAYEMS month-over-month change vs 5-day forward SPX returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation exceeds 3%, it triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy, which depresses equity valuations and leads to negative 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: If the average 60-day forward return following at least 5 trigger events is not significantly negative (one-sample t-test p > 0.10), the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth is strong but the fed funds rate is simultaneously rising, the positive demand signal is offset by monetary tightening, resulting in muted or negative forward returns over the 20-day horizon. The interaction of high demand and tightening policy creates an unreliable positive signal.
failure_condition: If the coefficient of the interaction term (RSAFS_growth * FEDFUNDS) in predicting 20-day returns is not significantly negative (p < 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window, or if the joint condition yields average returns not significantly lower than the unconditional mean, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-interest-rate regime (FEDFUNDS < 2%), rising housing starts signal robust construction activity and economic momentum, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days. This is a regime-conditional signal where the low-rate environment amplifies the positive effect of housing starts.
failure_condition: If the average 60-day forward return following HOUST growth > 0% month-over-month in periods when FEDFUNDS < 2% is not significantly positive (one-sample t-test p > 0.10) over at least 5 non-overlapping episodes, reject.
